title: Prefix vs Postfix Trace
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read one integer and print the results of a series of prefix and postfix increment/decrement operations applied to it.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N

## Output
Print exactly five lines in this order:
- `start: <N>` — original value
- `post-inc result: <value>` — value returned by `n++`
- `after post-inc: <n>` — value of n after the postfix increment
- `pre-dec result: <value>` — value returned by `--n`
- `after pre-dec: <n>` — value of n after the prefix decrement

## Requirements
1. After reading N, apply `n++` and capture its returned value separately from n.
2. Then apply `--n` and capture its returned value separately.
3. N fits in an `int`.
4. Edge case: N = 0 should produce `post-inc result: 0`, `after post-inc: 1`, `pre-dec result: 0`, `after pre-dec: 0`.

test_cases.label: Sample: N=5
test_cases.expected_stdout:
start: 5
post-inc result: 5
after post-inc: 6
pre-dec result: 5
after pre-dec: 5

test_cases.label: Hidden: N=0
test_cases.expected_stdout:
start: 0
post-inc result: 0
after post-inc: 1
pre-dec result: 0
after pre-dec: 0

test_cases.label: Hidden: N=-3
test_cases.expected_stdout:
start: -3
post-inc result: -3
after post-inc: -2
pre-dec result: -3
after pre-dec: -3

title: Next and Previous Values
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `nextValue(int x)` that returns `x` incremented by 1 using prefix `++`, and the function `prevValue(int x)` that returns `x` decremented by 1 using prefix `--`.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N

## Output
Print exactly two lines:
- `next: <value>` — return value of `nextValue(N)`
- `prev: <value>` — return value of `prevValue(N)`

## Requirements
1. Implement `nextValue(int x)` using `++x` (prefix increment) and returning the result.
2. Implement `prevValue(int x)` using `--x` (prefix decrement) and returning the result.
3. N fits in an `int`.
4. Edge case: N = 0 should print `next: 1` and `prev: -1`.
5. Edge case: N = -5 should print `next: -4` and `prev: -6`.
